agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A production logistics system enforces a strict 1:1 Shipment-to-Carrier assignment with supervisor-only reassignment. The product team requests an auto-split feature that would automatically divide overweight shipments across multiple carriers without supervisor involvement.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of cargo to be delivered, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier at all times post-assignment."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The entity responsible for transporting a Shipment."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept — a portion of an original Shipment created by the auto-split process. Does not yet exist in the domain model."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"A privileged role that is the only actor authorized to reassign a Carrier on a Shipment."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed automatic process that divides a Shipment exceeding 500 kg into multiple sub-shipments, each assigned to a different Carrier."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment Lock","definition":"The existing invariant that once a Carrier is assigned, only a Supervisor can change it."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The point at which a Shipment is handed off for transport; the system guarantees exactly one Carrier is assigned at this moment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data integrity and state consistency constraint. If violated, the system cannot determine who is responsible for a shipment, breaking chain of custody, liability, and tracking.","testable_assertion":"For every Shipment in any state from 'assigned' through 'delivered', COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization integrity constraint. Without it, carrier assignments become unstable and untraceable, breaking auditability and operational control.","testable_assertion":"Any Carrier reassignment event on a Shipment must have an associated Supervisor actor in the audit log."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At the time of dispatch, every Shipment has exactly one Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — a shipment on a truck is on exactly one truck. This is the ultimate consistency check before real-world handoff.","testable_assertion":"No Shipment may transition to 'dispatched' status unless COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A shipment is exactly 500 kg — does it split or not?","expected_behavior":"Undefined. The threshold boundary condition is unspecified."},{"scenario":"A shipment is 1500 kg — does it split into 3 sub-shipments of 500 kg each, or 2 of 750 kg?","expected_behavior":"Undefined. The splitting algorithm and sub-shipment sizing rules are not specified."},{"scenario":"A carrier is already assigned to the original shipment before auto-split triggers — what happens to that locked assignment?","expected_behavior":"Contradiction. The existing assignment is locked (BR-2), but auto-split must reassign or remove it without supervisor involvement."},{"scenario":"One sub-shipment fails delivery but others succeed — what is the status of the original shipment?","expected_behavior":"Undefined. The parent-child lifecycle relationship between Shipment and Sub-shipment does not exist in the model."},{"scenario":"A sub-shipment itself exceeds 500 kg due to indivisible cargo items","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Recursive splitting or exception handling is not addressed."},{"scenario":"Insurance, customs, or billing is tied to the original shipment ID","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Splitting a shipment may break downstream references in billing, customs declarations, and insurance policies that assume a 1:1 shipment identity."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High risk. Splitting shipments affects billing (who pays for each sub-shipment?), insurance (coverage per sub-shipment vs. original), and carrier contracts (rate structures may not support partial loads). Incorrect implementation could create revenue leakage or liability gaps.","operational":"Fundamental model change. Introducing a parent-child shipment hierarchy changes tracking, reporting, SLA measurement, and exception handling across the entire operation. This is not a feature — it is a domain model evolution.","user":"Supervisors lose oversight of carrier assignment for a critical class of shipments (heavy ones, which are often the most valuable). Carriers receive partial loads without the operational context of the whole shipment. Customers tracking a shipment now must track multiple sub-shipments.","risk":"CRITICAL. The feature as specified directly contradicts two production invariants. Implementing it as described would corrupt the domain model. Additionally, the 500 kg threshold is a policy decision presented as a hard requirement with no justification or configurability mentioned.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Loss of authority over carrier reassignment for auto-split shipments. Their control invariant (BR-2) is bypassed."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"Receive partial shipments without full context. Liability and handoff procedures undefined for sub-shipments."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Shipment tracking experience changes from single-shipment to multi-sub-shipment. Delivery coordination becomes more complex."},{"group":"Finance/Billing","impact":"Rate calculation, invoicing, and revenue recognition must handle split shipments. No rules defined."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal","impact":"Chain of custody, insurance coverage, and customs documentation for split shipments are undefined."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Requires fundamental domain model evolution — new aggregate (Sub-shipment or ShipmentGroup), new lifecycle rules, new events. Not a simple feature addition."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The feature request contains a direct contradiction with two established production invariants (1:1 Carrier assignment and Supervisor-only reassignment). Additionally, the request is vague on splitting logic, sub-shipment lifecycle, and downstream impacts. Confidence is low because the spec as written cannot be implemented without either violating existing invariants or fundamentally redefining core domain concepts — and the request does not acknowledge this.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction between the feature request and existing invariants BR-1 and BR-2 is unambiguous","The 500 kg threshold is a policy (invariant: false), not a physical constraint","This requires a domain model evolution, not just a feature addition"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends to relax existing invariants or introduce a new aggregate concept","How sub-shipments relate to billing, insurance, and compliance","Whether 'without supervisor involvement' means no human oversight at all or just no manual carrier selection"],"assumptions":["The existing invariants (BR-1, BR-2, BR-3) are correctly stated and currently enforced in production","The product team has not yet discussed relaxing these invariants","Sub-shipment is a new concept not yet present in the domain model"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature directly violates BR-1 (one Shipment = one Carrier). After splitting, the original shipment concept is associated with multiple carriers via sub-shipments. Either (a) we redefine 'Shipment' to allow a parent-child hierarchy where each child still has exactly one Carrier, preserving BR-1 at the sub-shipment level, or (b) we relax BR-1 to allow multi-carrier shipments. These are fundamentally different domain models.","affected_stakeholders":["Engineering","Supervisors","Carriers","Customers"],"options":["Introduce a ShipmentGroup/ParentShipment aggregate that contains multiple Sub-shipments, each respecting BR-1 individually","Relax BR-1 to allow a Shipment to have multiple Carriers with a lead-carrier concept","Reject the feature as incompatible with the domain model"],"recommendation":"Option A — introduce a ShipmentGroup aggregate. Each sub-shipment is a first-class Shipment with its own Carrier assignment, preserving BR-1. The ShipmentGroup is a new aggregate that tracks the relationship and lifecycle of the whole. This requires explicit domain modeling before implementation."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"The feature bypasses BR-2 (Supervisor-only reassignment) by automatically assigning different carriers to sub-shipments without supervisor involvement. This breaks the authorization invariant that exists for operational control and auditability.","affected_stakeholders":["Supervisors","Compliance/Legal"],"options":["Require supervisor approval for auto-split (defeats the 'automatic' requirement but preserves BR-2)","Create a new authorization rule: system-initiated splits during initial assignment are exempt from BR-2, but post-assignment changes still require supervisor","Introduce an 'Auto-split Policy' that supervisors pre-approve, delegating authority to the system within defined parameters"],"recommendation":"Option C — let supervisors define auto-split policies (weight thresholds, approved carrier pools, max sub-shipments). The system executes within those pre-approved boundaries. This preserves the spirit of BR-2 (supervisor control) while enabling automation."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"The splitting algorithm, sub-shipment sizing rules, carrier selection criteria, and parent-child lifecycle are entirely undefined. Without these, acceptance criteria cannot be written.","affected_stakeholders":["Engineering","Product"],"options":["Request a detailed spec from product covering: splitting algorithm, carrier selection rules, sub-shipment lifecycle, failure handling, and billing model","Define a minimal viable split (equal weight distribution, round-robin carrier selection) and iterate"],"recommendation":"Request the detailed spec. This is too consequential a domain change to iterate without clear business rules."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple carriers to what was a single shipment without introducing a new aggregate concept","business_rule_broken":"BR-1: A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier"},{"violation":"Auto-split bypasses supervisor authority for carrier assignment decisions","business_rule_broken":"BR-2: Only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier on a Shipment"},{"violation":"Feature request does not define how the dispatch invariant applies to split shipments","business_rule_broken":"BR-3: At dispatch, every Shipment has exactly one Carrier"}]}
